name: Four-square layout
description: Floor-layout strategy that partitions a square footprint into a 2x2 grid of four equally sized rooms separated by a cruciform pair of internal walls, with a central feature (chimney stack, masonry fireplace block, or compact stair shaft) anchoring the intersection where the two walls cross. Each quadrant typically receives one primary domestic function — kitchen, living room, bedroom, dining room — so a single floor delivers the full programme of a small dwelling without corridors. The geometry is the defining trait of the American Foursquare house and of Georgian colonial cottages, and is prized for its uncompromising symmetry, straightforward framing, and the way the central mass radiates heat into all four rooms at once.
